370	    \item[\textbf{($\alpha$)}] \emph{Hilbert / Quot scheme infrastructure plus FGA representability}: unlocks Route A. The single highest-cost build-out, since Hilbert/Quot schemes are upstream of much of modern algebraic geometry; but once present, the rest of Route A (identity component, smoothness, properness, universal property) follows by classical arguments already widely formalised in adjacent settings.
371	    \item[\textbf{($\beta$)}] \emph{Symmetric powers of schemes plus finite-group-scheme quotients plus Stein factorisation for proper morphisms (with its cohomology-of-$\mathcal O_X$-modules hypotheses)}: unlocks Route B. Each piece is a meaningful Mathlib contribution in its own right; the bottleneck is the smooth-quotient property of $C^g / S_g$.
372	    \item[\textbf{($\gamma$)}] \emph{The rigidity theorem for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, over an arbitrary base field $k$}: this is the prerequisite of the genus-$0$ sub-case under either Route A or Route B. The project packages it as \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}; the Lean identifier still carries the iter-126 over-$\bar k$ filename but its signature is already $k$-agnostic), gated on the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}: cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $k$ via the functorial shear iso; scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec k$ via the $k$-agnostic \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass; characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$. Per the iter-127 over-k commitment (\texttt{analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md}), each piece builds directly over $k$ with no algebraic-closure, perfect-base, or Galois-descent prerequisite. The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence is packaged as \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}), which invokes \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} directly with the supplied $k$-rational marked point $P \in C(k)$ in the $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ branch and is vacuously true on the $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$). Independently of ($\alpha$) and ($\beta$), establishing ($\gamma$) lets the genus-$0$ part of the witness be discharged unconditionally; the higher-genus part of the witness then remains under ($\alpha$) or ($\beta$).
373	  \end{itemize}
374	
375	  In sum: Mathlib currently contains none of the three infrastructure builds, and so the existence statement is recorded here as the project's single explicit foundational hypothesis. It backs all downstream consequences --- the four protected instances on $\Jac(C)$ (Theorems~\ref{thm:Jacobian_grpObj}--\ref{thm:Jacobian_geomIrred}), and the Abel--Jacobi map and its universal property of Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi} --- and is discharged by any one of the three routes above once the corresponding Mathlib infrastructure lands.
376	\end{proof}
377	
378	\subsection{The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence}
379	\label{sec:genusZeroWitness}
380	
381	The genus-$0$ sub-case of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} admits an explicit witness construction at the Lean level, packaged separately from the general existence statement so that the genus-$0$ branch can be discharged independently once the shared cotangent-vanishing pile lands (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii)).
382	
383	\begin{theorem}[Genus-$0$ Albanese witness]
384	  \label{def:genusZeroWitness}
385	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}
386	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness, thm:rigidity_over_kbar, def:genus}
387	  \notready
388	  Let $C \in \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then there exists a $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\,C$ whose underlying scheme is $\Spec k$ (the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$), with the trivial group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension $0$, properness, and geometric irreducibility, and whose $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}$ field encodes the Albanese universal property for every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$.
389	\end{theorem}
390	
391	\begin{proof}
392	  \uses{thm:rigidity_over_kbar, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen, def:IsAlbanese, def:JacobianWitness, def:genus}
393	  \leanok
394	  We construct the seven fields of \cref{def:JacobianWitness}.
395	
396	  \paragraph{Underlying scheme.} Take $J := \mathbf{1}$, the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, which on underlying schemes is $\Spec k$ viewed as a $k$-scheme via the identity structure morphism.
397	
398	  \paragraph{Group-object structure, properness, smoothness, geometric irreducibility.} The terminal object $\mathbf{1}$ of any cartesian-monoidal category carries the (unique) trivial group-object structure: multiplication and inverse are the unique morphisms to $\mathbf{1}$, and the identity element is the identity morphism. The structure morphism $\mathbf{1}.\mathtt{hom} = \mathrm{id}_{\Spec k}$ is proper (the identity is a closed immersion, in particular proper), smooth (the identity is unramified and flat, hence smooth), and geometrically irreducible. The geometric-irreducibility instance is the project-local helper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.geometricallyIrreducible\_id\_Spec} (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:120}--\texttt{126}), which derives geometric irreducibility of the identity on $\Spec k$ from the fact that, on the geometric fibre $\Spec K$ over any field extension $k \to K$, the second projection of the pullback square is an isomorphism, and $\Spec K$ is irreducible.
399	
400	  \paragraph{Smoothness of relative dimension $\genus\,C$.} The smoothness witness $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ is required at relative dimension $\genus\,C$. The hypothesis $\genus\,C = 0$ rewrites the target relative dimension to $0$, after which the goal reduces to $\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 0\ (\mathrm{id}_{\Spec k})$. This is a Mathlib instance: the identity morphism on any base is smooth of relative dimension $0$.
401	
402	  \paragraph{The \texttt{isAlbaneseFor} field.} For every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$, we exhibit an \cref{def:IsAlbanese} term for the triple $(C, P, J)$.
403	
404	  The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map $\alpha := \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \colon C \to \mathbf{1}$ (the structure morphism of $C$ as a $k$-scheme, factored through the terminal object). The pointed condition $P \circ \alpha = \eta_J = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}$ holds automatically: both sides are morphisms $\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, and the terminal object has a unique endomorphism.
405	
406	  For the universal factorisation: given a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $A$ over $k$ with identity element $\eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$ and a morphism $f \colon C \to A$ with $P \circ f = \eta_A$, we must exhibit a unique morphism $g \colon J \to A$ such that $f = \alpha \circ g$. \emph{Existence}: take $g := \eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$. Then $\alpha \circ g = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$, which equals $f$ precisely when $f$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. This is the content of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (the project's named rigidity declaration of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, established directly over the base field $k$ per the iter-127 over-k commitment): the supplied marked point $P \in C(k)$ feeds the pointing hypothesis, and the rigidity conclusion is exactly $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$ as scheme morphisms. \emph{Uniqueness}: any two morphisms $\mathbf{1} \to A$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ agreeing on $P \circ (-) = \eta_A$ are equal by the universal property of the terminal object $\mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ as a category (a morphism out of $\mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ is determined by where it sends the unique generator, here pinned to $\eta_A$).
407	
408	  \paragraph{Vacuity for the $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch.} When no $k$-rational point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$ exists (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexample), the universal quantifier $\forall (P : \mathbf{1} \to C), \mathtt{IsAlbanese}\,C\,P\,J$ is vacuously satisfied at the Lean type level: Lean's $\forall$ over an empty type is trivially true. The construction handles this branch implicitly without any explicit case split.
409	
410	  \paragraph{Body closure status.} The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:174}--\texttt{178}) is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is gated on the body of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (M2.a), which in turn depends on pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}) landing in iter-129+. Earliest realistic body-closure iteration: iter-138+.
411	\end{proof}
412	
413	\section{Implementation route via the Albanese functor}
414	
415	Because the full Picard-scheme representability theorem is not available in current Mathlib, the formal construction proceeds in two layers:
416	
417	\begin{enumerate}
418	  \item \textbf{Layer I --- direct definition.} The Jacobian of $C$ is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of the Albanese witness provided by Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, carrying the four properties of Theorems~\ref{thm:Jacobian_grpObj}--\ref{thm:Jacobian_geomIrred} (each obtained by projection from the witness). The genus-$0$ case is included: the witness's universal property in that case carries the rigidity content for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme. Per the iter-127 over-k commitment (\texttt{analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md}), this rigidity is established directly over the base field $k$ via the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} and the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii); no base-change to $\bar k$ and no Galois descent of morphism equality enters. The genus-$0$ arm is packaged as the explicit Lean construction \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}: the underlying scheme is the terminal object $\Spec k$, the universal morphism in the $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ branch is the unique terminal map \texttt{toUnit}\,$C$, and the universal factorisation reduces to \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}.
419	
420	  \item \textbf{Layer II --- Abel--Jacobi compatibility.} Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi} introduces the Abel--Jacobi morphism $C \to \Jac(C)$ and shows that it is universal among morphisms from $C$ to abelian varieties.
421	\end{enumerate}
Tasks tracked. Writing the Jacobian.tex writer directive then dispatching all 4 Wave 2 subagents.

   bodied scaffold landing). That gives the same "do the pieces
   compose at the type level" signal as the temporary-axiom smoke
   test, without introducing any axiom.

   tilts the wrong way.** A failure detected at iter-143 (via temporary
   axiom) vs. iter-151 (via real piece (iii)) saves 8 iter of build,
   per the strategy-critic. But: the iter-143 plan agent would have to
   stage the temporary-axiom infrastructure + commit it + remove it
   + audit the removal — each step a new failure surface. The
   iter-135 plan agent's judgment is that the lighter-weight

   axioms) gives 80% of the integration signal for 20% of the
   process cost. The full-strength integration signal (the actual
   composition compiles) is only available at the real iter-151+
   close anyway.

4. **The CONVERGING verdict on Route 1 + the 1-CHALLENGE SOUND verdict
   on the rest of the strategy reinforces this.** The iter-135
   progress-critic + strategy-critic are aligned that the strategy is
   healthy; iter-143 is far enough out that a deeper-think trigger
   then can reconsider, but the iter-135 pre-commitment is not to
   schedule a temporary-axiom lane.
